name: A Cozinha Brasileira
description: O Cozinheiro Nacional (c. 1874) — the classic collection of Brazilian and European recipes.

name: A Cozinha Portuguesa
description: Portuguese cookery from Cozinheiro moderno (1785) and Arte de cozinha (1834).

name: A Magyar Konyha
description: Hungarian cookery from the Képes budapesti szakácskönyv (1901).

name: De Hollandse Keuken
description: Dutch home cooking from Betje de goedkoope keukenmeid (1895) and the Amsterdam Huishoudschool cookbook (1910).

name: Det Danske Køkken
description: Danish cookery from Frøken Jensen (1921) and illustrated household cookbooks, 1884-1899.

name: Det Norske Kjøkken
description: Skandinavisk-amerikansk kogebog (1884) — Dano-Norwegian immigrant cooking, shared Scandinavian heritage labeled honestly.

name: Die Deutsche Küche
description: German home cooking from Volks-Kochbuch (Hedwig Heyl) and the Süddeutsches Kochbuch.

name: Die Wiener Küche
description: Viennese cookery from Wiener Kochbücher, 1825-1914.

name: Kuchnia Polska
description: Polish cooking from Kuchnia polsko-amerykańska (1917).

name: La Cocina Argentina
description: Juana Manuela Gorriti's Cocina Ecléctica (1890) — recipes gathered from women across Argentina and South America.

name: La Cocina Chilena
description: Novísimo manual del cocinero práctico chileno (1896).

name: La Cocina Española
description: Spanish cookery from El Practicón (1894) and La mesa moderna (1888).

name: La Cocina Guatemalteca
description: Consejos prácticos sobre cocina al estilo del país (1922).

name: La Cocina Mexicana
description: Mexican cookery from the Diccionario de cocina (1845) and La cocinera poblana (1890).

name: La Cucina Italiana
description: Pellegrino Artusi's La scienza in cucina e l'arte di mangiar bene (1891) — the book that unified Italian cooking.

name: La Cuisine Française
description: French cookery from the great tradition: La Cuisinière bourgeoise (1801) and Le Cuisinier impérial (1806).

name: Louisiana Creole Kitchen
description: New Orleans Creole cooking from Lafcadio Hearn's La Cuisine Creole and The Creole Cookery Book (both 1885).

name: Suomen Keittiö
description: Finnish cooking from Finnish-American keittokirjat, 1914-1928.

name: Svenska Köket
description: Swedish home cooking from Hemmets kokbok and Iduns kokbok (both 1911).

name: The Australian Table
description: Australian cookery 1864-1930, from the goldfields era to Federation home cooking.

name: The Canadian Kitchen
description: Canadian home cooking from farm and family cookbooks, 1911-1915.

name: The Chinese Kitchen
description: Early Chinese-American cookbooks: Shiu Wong Chan (1917) and Jessie Louise Nolton (1911).

name: The Filipino Kitchen
description: The Manila Cook Book (1919), American-era Manila home cooking.

name: The Indian Kitchen
description: Recipes from British-India cookbooks, 1880-1900 — a corpus shared across the subcontinent (India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka).

name: The Japanese Kitchen
description: The Chinese-Japanese Cook Book (1914) — one of the first Japanese cookbooks in English. Shared Chinese-Japanese heritage, labeled honestly.

name: The Jewish Kitchen
description: European Jewish cookery from The Jewish Manual (1846) to the International Jewish Cook Book (1918).

name: The Ottoman Turkish Kitchen
description: Turabi Efendi's Turkish Cookery Book (1864), Ottoman recipes in English.

name: The Victorian British Kitchen
description: Mrs Beeton's Book of Household Management (1861) — the defining cookbook of Victorian Britain.

name: The West Indies Kitchen
description: The Nabob's Cookery Book (1900) — East and West Indian recipes, shared colonial corpus labeled honestly.

name: Ye Old American Recipe Book
description: Recipes from American cookery books published 1796-1896, all public domain.

name: Česká Kuchyně
description: Czech cookery from Česká kuchařka (1883) and Czech-American kuchařky, 1896-1904.
